title: Uniface Messages
text: Uniface Messages Understand how Uniface messages are used (for example, message severity levels, message numbering scheme, or how message text is displayed). Uniface messages have the following severity levels: Error messages—an action or event has caused an error in the application, preventing the action or event from continuing. Warning messages—an action or event is likely to cause an error in the application, or if data is likely to be lost. Information messages—provide feedback on the success or failure of an action or event, and provide information on the status of the application. Uniface message share the same four-digit numbering scheme. You can use these numbers to refer to the documentation for information on the possible causes and solutions for errors and warnings, and for explanations of information messages. Messages might be displayed in a pop-up dialog or in the Message Frame. Refer to the following topics: Uniface Messages 0000-0999 Uniface Messages 1000-1999 Uniface Messages 2000-2999 Uniface Messages 3000-3999 Uniface Messages 4000-4999 Uniface Messages 5000-5999 Uniface Messages 6000-6999 Uniface Messages 7000-7999 Uniface Messages 8000-8999 Uniface Messages 9000-9999 Was this topic helpful?
